source.title: How To Setup and Defend Kanal (All Sites) - Rainbow Six Siege
source.notes: Creator (aLittleTon) walks through all four Kanal defense sites: Server/Control (Top Floor), Basement, Maps/Security, and Coast Guard/Gym (in-game called 'Lounge'). Generic ranked setups, not pro-league lineups. No corresponding Attack transcript was available for this map, so this file is Defense-only. Room/callout names are the creator's informal spoken callouts (e.g. 'printer' / 'New Bridge', 'Kayaks', 'Old Bridge') and need reconciling against data/maps/kanal.json once wiki callouts are confirmed. Compass directions could not be reliably grounded against a real Kanal blueprint (no accessible top-down source found — see report), so locations are room/feature names only, without compass prefixes. ASR is generally clean on operator names for this transcript, with a couple of exceptions noted inline on the relevant items.

reinforcements.location: Far left-hand side of the back Server wall

reinforcements.note: Sixth and seventh reinforcements, on the main breach into the site.

headHoles.note: Never reinforce. Watch feet as attackers close the distance from Printer/New Bridge. Also peeks the breach; holds Printer or New Bridge from here.

headHoles.location: Door Wall Toward Printer, Feet Holes
headHoles.note: Cut feet holes all the way across instead of reinforcing. Play prone/crouched further back to catch feet without exposing your head. Still peek the door angle from the side of the hole.

headHoles.location: High Head Hole Near Desk, Top Red Side
headHoles.note: Stops the C4 plant on the far end of the desk.

headHoles.location: Head Hole Onto Top Red, Over Desk
headHoles.note: Holds top red, sees anyone walking in, also covers breach and plant. Well-known angle — treat as a late-round surprise rather than sitting in it openly.

headHoles.location: Small Punch Hole on Floor Near Top Green
headHoles.note: Holds Printer Bridge/New Bridge from a spot attackers don't expect checked. Reliable one-tap with ACOG.

rotates.note: Pair with the high head hole to stop the far-desk C4 plant.

hatches.location: Hatch Near Default Plant Spot (this side of the green wall)
hatches.note: Leave open. Lets a Bandit-type player drop below to hold the breach from underneath. Ask a teammate to open it if it isn't already.

gadgetPlacements.location: Top Red Stairs, Top Red Window, Corridor Window
gadgetPlacements.note: Place three jammers: top red stairs, corridor window, and tight on the nearby window. Blocks drones down the whole corridor so a shotgun-holder there can't be scouted before you commit.

gadgetPlacements.location: Printer / New Bridge One-Way Angle, Signage and Unistrut Grid
gadgetPlacements.note: Source audio says 'Kayo' and 'Kayo barriers' with a 5-barrier loadout — does not match any real Kanal defender. Closest real candidates are Kaid (Electroclaw, not a see-through barrier) or Melusi; treat operator identity as unconfirmed. Setup: throw two barriers stacked near the 'Westwing' sign or on the bottom row of the Unistrut grid (not the middle) to build a one-way wall covering Printer while staying hidden; third barrier on the bomb to block the breach angle.

gadgetPlacements.location: Pipes (Basement), viewed via hatch from Control/Server
gadgetPlacements.note: Camera watches Pipes below to time a C4 kill through the floor — see Basement site entry for full setup.

operatorRecommendations.note: Plays the never-reinforce wall near New Bridge/Printer catching feet as attackers close in. Can drop through the plant-side hatch to hold the breach from below.
Referred to as 'Cade' in source audio. Electroclaws the reinforced breach wall for a safer, more protected hold.
Runs three jammers across top red stairs and the corridor windows, then holds a shotgun angle in the corridor safe from drones.

generalTips.note: Top-red head hole and the feet-hole wall together force attackers to worry about multiple angles at once instead of clearing one at a time.
Don't reinforce both the feet-hole wall and the neighbouring wall — leaves attackers with only the door to worry about. Keep the feet-hole wall soft.
After a plant, come up an alternate route (not the obvious red stairs door) and go prone for an unexpected angle on the planter behind the desk.
If the feet-hole wall can't be pushed through directly, loop around and re-enter via the open breach for a free kill on the planter — strong in 1v1/1v2 retakes.
Use the Aim Labs range on Kanal to warm up before the round starts.
Top green is a really important anchor spot — holds green stairs and two windows.
The corridor position near the Mute jammers is busy, not fully safe — but guaranteed safe from being droned. Trade there and burn attacker drone economy first.
The default C4 plant spot is on this side of the green wall near the open hatch.

reinforcements.note: Reinforce rather than feet-hole. This wall sits too close to an external door — Ash charges or Zofia impacts (source audio: 'zapir') can blow it from outside unseen.

reinforcements.location: Site Hatch Upstairs in Coast Guard

rotates.note: Leave soft. Cut a line of sight here plus a second one nearby so a defender covers both the angle and the door from one position.

rotates.location: Supply Room, Right Side — Hole Toward Main Stairs
rotates.note: Cut into the soft right side of the Supply/Kayaks wall. Holds Main Stairs from the back of site and sees the default plant through it; catches attackers turning the corner from Main Stairs.

hatches.note: Leave open/soft, not reinforced. Gives a roamer stuck above (e.g. Lesion or Alibi) a way back into the fight instead of being forced down Main/Yellow Stairs or the harder side-hatch drop. Also lets you hold Gym/Lock window from above. Only two angles to cover if someone drops through: down Shower Corridor, or into site.

gadgetPlacements.note: Place camera in Pipes; drop a C4 in the same spot. Sit on the camera, wait for someone to walk past, blow the C4. Clear nearby breakables first so you can see through the cabinet gap.

gadgetPlacements.location: Anchor Spot Near Trench-Door Angle (no vertical above it)
gadgetPlacements.note: Hold with a couple of ADS deployed. Also a good spot to drop C4 into site if needed.

operatorRecommendations.note: Runs the Pipes camera + C4 combo for a near-automatic kill most rounds.
Holds the no-vertical anchor spot near the trench door with ADS support.
Benefits from the open Gym hatch as a route back down into the fight if caught upstairs.

generalTips.note: Second-strongest site on the map behind Server/Control, though most Kanal sites are solid.
A defender upstairs at this site should actively cause delay, not sit passive, and always have a retreat plan down the stairs.
Leave attackers with at least two angles to worry about at the critical entry point rather than one clearable angle plus a door.

reinforcements.location: Two Walls Near Retake Fallback Area
reinforcements.note: Two of the remaining reinforcements once set up.

headHoles.location: Low Head Hole Near Hatch/Old Bridge Area
headHoles.note: Keep deliberately below head height so it isn't obvious. Punishes anyone dropping the nearby hatch from above (vestibule area), or keep firing into that area even if the drop shot is missed.

rotates.note: Uses the wall's concrete as cover. Without it, Kitchen can only be held from one door or the hatch; with it, multiple angles to peek Kitchen plus a quick way back into site.

rotates.location: Impact Wall Rotate Plus Second Nearby Rotate
rotates.note: Two standard rotates between site and the corridor.
Lets a defender come through Security and hold the Kitchen door without going through the door itself.

hatches.note: Leave open by default — mainly useful for a stuck roamer to drop back via Red or Green Stairs / Old Bridge. Reinforcing instead is fine if no one is playing above.

gadgetPlacements.location: Corridor Into Site (covers Red, Green stairs/Museum, Old Bridge, Kitchen approaches)
gadgetPlacements.note: Place three Evil Eye cameras to catch nearly every drone entering the site — almost all approaches funnel through this corridor. Cameras also give plant/security audio intel where they can't see directly. Use for intel/drone destruction only — fighting players with the laser burns the camera for minimal damage and loses information.

gadgetPlacements.location: Door at End of Corridor (Old Bridge side)
gadgetPlacements.note: Barricade this door and punch through it twice at the base — attackers can't tell it's already been punched. A roamer caught on the wrong side of the bridge can punch through fully and get back quickly; the extra punch also gives sound/timing intel on approaching attackers.

operatorRecommendations.note: Site setup built around a 3-camera Evil Eye web covering the single main corridor into the site; sits on the crouch/table-cover angle watching both hatch-drop spots and Old Bridge.

generalTips.note: Always keep someone playing above, even just to delay — if defense loses map control above this site, attackers can open the entire floor and defenders below have nowhere to hide.
All of the floor across this site and into Security is soft — assume it's fully frag/breach-able from below/above.
Nearly every drone into the site funnels down one corridor (via Red, Green stairs/Museum, or Old Bridge), which is why the 3-camera Maestro web is effective here.

headHoles.location: Printer-Facing Side Door, First Head Hole
headHoles.note: Come out of the door and hold toward Printer and the side door, then C4 the plant from behind the bomb if attackers control Printer.

headHoles.location: Printer-Facing Side Door, Second Head Hole
headHoles.note: Extends coverage from Gym with an ACOG/DMR-friendly long angle onto the Printer door.

headHoles.location: Angle Onto Main Stairs and Down the Corridor
headHoles.note: Bonus angle available from the same head-hole position.

rotates.location: Back of Gym Rotate, Far Right Side of Wall
rotates.note: Not everyone likes this rotate, but if the only way out of Gym is this rotate or the hatch, being able to fight into the corridor from here is valuable.

hatches.note: Leave open (mirrors the same hatch discussed in the Basement site). Keep it open for an escape route out of Gym if pushed.

gadgetPlacements.location: Pipes (Basement), viewed via camera from this site's rotate
gadgetPlacements.note: Same camera + C4 combo used at Server/Control and Basement, visible from this site's back rotate.

gadgetPlacements.location: Top of Stairs, Side of Stairs (Coast Guard/Gym approach)
gadgetPlacements.note: Source audio says 'maloi'/'M' phonetically, best matching Melusi given the described Banshee-style trap on the stairs for an audio warning before attackers arrive from behind. Transcript also describes her running 'with an ACOG', which doesn't match Melusi's real loadout — treat as unconfirmed/likely creator imprecision.

operatorRecommendations.note: Best played with a DMR-capable loadout to hold two long angles from Gym (toward the printer door and down the corridor); places her Banshee trap on the stairs for a flank warning.
Runs the same Pipes camera + C4 combo used on the other sites, visible from this site's back rotate.

generalTips.note: Tertiary site — very strong with an organized/coordinated squad, difficult to defend without coordination since it's rarely practiced.
Never let the feet-hole wall near Main Stairs go fully soft as a straight wall-bang — an attacker can grenade a defender tucked behind it and force them out, handing over Printer.
Keep an escape route open (e.g. the open Gym hatch) — if pushed by three attackers, drop down and rotate back up Main Stairs instead of getting trapped.
A basement player watching Main Stairs from below covers this site's vertical threat — attackers commonly push through Trench and straight up the stairs.

Feet holes near Main Stairs let a defender see feet of anyone crossing into Gym or Coast Guard — desks partially block sightlines into the site itself, so don't rely on seeing everything through them.
